hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Only the unique string value itself is retained, but if the same string is 'sent' multiple times, only the terminal 'send' operation for that specific string label is kept as the replay value (all others are overwritten or discarded).

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_state_reduction.json.description: A state container is created with an initial state and a set of action-to-state reducers. As soon as a listener is attached it receives the initial state, and thereafter every processed action is reduced to a new state which is delivered to the listener in order. Each emitted state is reported on its own line using its opaque state label.

feature1_2_buffered_replay.json.description: States produced before any listener is attached are buffered in order, and the buffer is replayed to the first listener the moment it attaches. The buffer always begins with the initial state, followed by one state per action processed while no listener was present. After the buffered states are replayed, subsequent processing continues to be delivered live.

feature1_3_single_event.json.description: A state may be declared as a single event: it is delivered to the listener exactly like any other state, but it is never retained as the container's current state. After a single event is emitted, the next reduction still observes the previously persisted (non-event) state, so processing the same regular action again yields the same result as before the single event occurred.

feature1_4_errors.json.description: The container rejects two illegal situations with normalized error signals. Processing an action that has no registered reducer is reported as an unhandled-action error together with the offending action label. Processing any action after the container has been torn down (its bindings cleared) is reported as an unsubscribed error. Any states delivered before the error still appear, then the normalized error terminates the run.

feature2_live_stream.json.description: A live observable stream delivers values to its subscriber only while the subscription is active. Values emitted before a subscriber attaches are not buffered and are never seen. Once a subscriber detaches, further values are not delivered to it. Each delivered value is reported on its own line.

feature3_1_replay_stream.json.description: A replaying observable stream remembers the latest value it has seen for each value type and replays the remembered value(s) to every new subscriber the instant it attaches, in addition to forwarding live values. When several values share the same type only the most recent one is retained. A value sent while a subscriber is attached is delivered live; after detaching and re-attaching, the retained latest value is replayed again. While no subscriber is attached, values are still recorded but not delivered.

feature3_2_replay_clear.json.description: The replaying stream can have its remembered values cleared. After a clear, a newly attaching subscriber receives nothing from the past, confirming the retained value was truly discarded rather than merely hidden. Values delivered live before the clear still appear in the output.

feature3_3_replay_single_event.json.description: When a state container is observed through a replaying stream, the latest regular state is replayed to a new subscriber, but single-event states are never retained for replay. Reducing an action to a regular state records it for replay; emitting a single event delivers it live only; on detaching and re-attaching, only the latest regular state is replayed while the single event is omitted.

feature4_media_player.json.description: A media-player state machine drives a fixed circular playlist of tracks through actions. The playlist, in order, is: Like Ooh-Ahh, Cheer up, TT, Knock Knock, Signal, One More Time, Likey, Heart Shaker, Candy Pop, What Is Love?, Wake Me Up, Dance The Night Away, BDZ, Yes Or Yes, The Best Thing I Ever Did. The machine starts stopped. A pause_play toggles between playing and paused: from a stopped or paused position it begins playing the current track (the first track when nothing has played yet); while already playing it pauses. A next moves forward one track (and starts playing), a previous moves back one track (and starts playing); both wrap around the ends of the playlist. A stop halts playback and resets the position so the next play starts from the first track. Each delivered state is reported on its own line: a playing state as the played track preceded by a label, a paused state, or a stopped state. Only states produced by processed actions are reported (the implicit initial stopped state is not).

_constraints.input_condition: Scenario is 'kaskade', listener is attached or about to be attached (subscribe op executed or simulated context for buffer)
_constraints.output_rule: Immediately emit the current persistent state label as the first output line

_constraints.input_condition: Action label exists in the 'reducers' map
_constraints.output_rule: Compute new state using the reducer's 'to' label. If new state differs from current, emit it. New state becomes current.

_constraints.input_condition: Action reduces to a state identical to the current state
_constraints.output_rule: Emit the state label again (duplicate line), preserving the stream flow even if no change occurred

_constraints.input_condition: Action label binds to a state present in the 'single_events' list
_constraints.output_rule: Emit the state label immediately, but do NOT update the current persistent state. The current state must remain the last valid (non-event) state for subsequent reductions.

_constraints.input_condition: Action label is provided but NOT present in the 'reducers' map
_constraints.output_rule: Terminate output stream. Append 'error=unhandled_action' on one line, followed by 'action=<label>' on the next line.

_constraints.input_condition: An action is processed after a 'teardown' op has been executed
_constraints.output_rule: Terminate output stream. Append 'error=unsubscribed' on one line (no separate action line)

_constraints.input_condition: Actions are processed BEFORE any 'subscribe' op
_constraints.output_rule: States are stored in an internal buffer. These states are NOT emitted until the first 'subscribe' op executes.

Determine the 'current' state at the moment of subscription (consisting of initial state + all processed actions prior to subscription). Emit ALL states from the buffer in order. Then proceed with live processing.

_constraints.feature: flow scenario live stream subscription check
_constraints.input_condition: Scenario is 'flow', kind is 'plain', 'subscribe' op executed
_constraints.output_rule: The internal observer is now attached. Subsequent 'send' ops deliver values. Prior 'send' ops (before subscribe) are discarded and not output.

_constraints.input_condition: Scenario is 'flow', kind is 'plain', 'unsubscribe' op executed
_constraints.output_rule: The internal observer is detached. Subsequent 'send' ops are dropped. No output generated for these sends.

_constraints.feature: flow scenario replay stream value retention
_constraints.input_condition: Kind is 'dam' (DAM). A 'send' op executes while subscribed or blindly.
_constraints.output_rule: Record the value. If a future subscriber attaches, replay the LATEST value recorded for that value type (string). Ignore older values of the same type.

_constraints.feature: flow scenario replay stream live emission
_constraints.input_condition: Kind is 'dam'. 'send' op executes while a subscriber is attached
_constraints.output_rule: Within the same event loop tick as subscription, deliver the value immediately (LIVE). Do not wait for subscription confirmation line (which does not exist for DAMs in this spec's output format description, they just output the value). *Correction based on test case logic*: The test case `feature3_1` shows `send` outputting immediately even before subscribe? Let's re-verify C011 and C012 based on test case feature3_1_case1_input: `ops`: send, send, subscribe, send. Output: world, hello. Here, SENDs before subscribe produced output. This implies 'send' with 'kind=dam' ALWAYS emits immediately if there is no active subscription requirement to wait, OR the 'subscribe' op in 'dam' mode triggers replay *and* maybe the first send is delayed? Wait, look closer at feature3_1_case1. `ops`: [send(test), send(world), subscribe, send(hello)]. Output: `world`, `hello`. The first `test` is lost. `world` (latest before subscribe) is replayed. `hello` (after subscribe) is sent live. This means 'send' in DAM mode DOES NOT emit immediately if unsubscribed. It emits ONLY: 1. If subscribed, as it happens. 2. If not subscribed, it records it. But `world` output IS `world`. Wait, the output sequence is `world` then `hello`. The `world` was sent *before* subscribe. So `send` in DAM mode emits if there is a pending buffer? No, logic in C011 says it records and replays. Logic in C012 says if subscribed, deliver live. The test case `feature3_1` output: `world` (from send before subscribe) comes first. Then `hello` (from send after subscribe). This implies the DAM stream replays the retained state *upon subscription*, but how did `world` appear? Ah, looking at `feature3_1_case1` again. It seems the spec implies `send` *does* produce output if it's the one that gets retained? NO, the buffer `test` is lost. `world` is retained. When `subscribe` happens, `world` is replayed. Then `hello` is sent live. So `send` ops generally do not output unless they are part of the retained set being replayed at attach time OR sent live. BUT wait, `feature3_2_case1`: ops [subscribe, send(hello), clear, unsubscribe, subscribe]. Output: `hello`. Here `hello` is sent, then clear happens. Then unsubscribe, then subscribe. Output is ONLY `hello`. If `clear` was in the middle, why didn't `hello` disappear immediately? Because `clear` only affects future subscribers. So `send` does NOT output immediately if unsubscribed. It only outputs when two conditions are met: 1. It is the 'latest' and subscriber attaches (replay trigger), OR 2. Subscriber is attached AND it happens live. So my previous C011 was incomplete. Refined: In DAM mode, `send` does not output immediately unless a subscriber is currently attached. Values are buffered if not subscribed. Upon subscription, the latest buffered value is replayed.
